text:
I'll search for this paper using the exact title to verify whether it exists.
The paper "MEGA: A Large-Scale Molecular Editing Dataset for Guided-Action Optimization" was found on OpenReview as a submission to the AI for Science workshop at NeurIPS 2025. However, I need to verify the author information more carefully, as the cited authors include names and an organization (Air Liquide) that appear unusual for an academic paper format.

Let me search for more information about the authors to verify if they match the actual paper.
Based on my web searches, I found the paper on OpenReview. The first search result shows the paper was found with the title "MEGA: A Large-Scale Molecular Editing Dataset for Guided-Action Optimization" on both OpenReview PDF (index 1-1) and the OpenReview forum (index 2-1, 2-2). The paper is confirmed to be submitted to the "Workshop: AI4Science-NeurIPS" for 2025 (as stated in index 1-11).

However, the OpenReview submission pages I found show the paper as "Anonymous Author(s)" (index 1-3), which is typical for papers under double-blind review. The actual author information from the cited reference (Nelson Fernandez, Air Liquide, Maxime Illouz, Luis Pinto, Entao Yang, Habiboulaye Amadou Boubacar) could not be verified from the search results because the OpenReview pages I accessed do not display the actual author names—they show "Anonymous Author(s)" due to the review process.

The exact title matches the paper found on OpenReview. The venue matches (AI4Science-NeurIPS 2025 Workshop). However, since the paper appears to be in double-blind review on OpenReview, the author names cannot be directly verified from the web search results. The cited author list appears plausible (including an Air Liquide affiliation which is a major chemical/materials company), and the paper itself exists.

EXPLANATION: The paper with this exact title exists on OpenReview as a 2025 workshop submission. However, the author information cannot be directly verified from accessible sources as the submission appears to be under double-blind review.
